mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand expansion that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing peak levels, which triggers short-term profit margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over one week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes yield positive 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of late-cycle expansion observations.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year CPI increase exceeding 2.5 percent forces expectations of aggressive Fed tightening, which raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly nonfarm payroll growth expands the consumer base and supports wage-driven revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show a Rank IC below 0.03 for 60-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing quarterly GDP growth and rising monthly retail sales indicates inventory accumulation ahead of a demand slowdown, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 growth and RSAFS changes fails to predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals demand destruction from higher borrowing costs, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes during tightening periods produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observations.
